item.title: Trump says 'everything's been knocked out' in Iran but offers no clear plan for war
item.description: Trump has faced criticism for conflicting messages about his justifications and aims after launching the region's biggest conflict in more than two decades.

item.title: Trump lashes out at Spain, says he is 'going to cut off all trade'
item.description: President Donald Trump said he wants to “cut off all trade with Spain” over NATO spending, adding “we don't want anything to do with Spain." His comments came during a meeting between him and German Chancellor Friedrich Merz.

item.title: Macron orders first evacuations of citizens as France boosts defences in the Middle East
item.description: France says it downed drones “in legitimate defence”, will send the warship to Cyprus and deploy air-defence assets to allies as the first two repatriation flights head to Paris on Tuesday night

item.title: 'Iran's attack will not go unanswered,' Qatar's foreign ministry says
item.description: Qatar's Foreign Ministry said Iran's attacks have crossed "every red line" and condemned the strikes which targeted civilian areas and facilities.

item.title: Latest news bulletin | March 3rd, 2026 – Evening
item.description: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this March 3rd, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

item.title: US could owe over €110bn in tariff refunds after court rejects Trump delay
item.description: After the US federal appeals court rejected Trump’s attempt to postpone proceedings on refunds for tariffs, the CIT must now establish a framework for what could become the largest tariff refund operation in US history.

item.title: What is Iran's Assembly of Experts and who will succeed Khamenei?
item.description: The death of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei has plunged Iran into a wartime leadership crisis. A temporary council runs the country while the Assembly of Experts — whose headquarters were hit in US-Israeli strikes on Tuesday — must select a successor.

item.title: France to facilitate the evacuation of 'vulnerable' nationals from Middle East
item.description: 400,000 French nationals are in countries across the Gulf affected by the Iran war, according to French Foreign Minister Jean-Noël Barrot.

item.title: Euronews correspondent reports from Doha as regional conflict continues into day four
item.description: Euronews correspondent Aadel Haleem in Doha reports on the latest developments as the conflict intensifies across the Middle East. Missiles were intercepted over the Qatari capital overnight, while airspace remains closed and shelter-in-place measures continue.

item.title: People lay flowers at makeshift Khamenei memorial in Moscow
item.description: Residents gathered outside the Iranian Embassy in Moscow to lay flowers and pray at a makeshift memorial for Iran’s late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

item.title: Tehran vows to strike European countries if they join Iran war
item.description: In an official announcement in Tehran on Tuesday, the Iranian foreign ministry spokesperson said that any action by Europeans will be regarded as an “act of war” and met with a response targeting Europe's cities.

item.title: Greece, France and Germany send forces to Cyprus after Iran-made drone strikes
item.description: Both Greece and France have agreed to send frigates to Cyprus after a UK air base was targeted by Iranian-made drones on Monday. France will also send additional land-based, anti-drone and anti-missile systems to the country.

item.title: Germany warned of Iranian sleeper cells after Tehran's fatwa on West
item.description: Experts in Germany have warned that Iran-linked sleeper cells could strike in Europe after a fatwa was issued by Tehran over the death of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, as Berlin boosts vigilance and US probes possible terror attacks.

item.title: Israelis celebrate Purim in bomb shelter amid missile alerts
item.description: Hundreds gathered in an underground parking shelter in Tel Aviv to celebrate Purim, as public events were restricted and missile alerts continued.

item.title: Images show damage at US Embassy in Riyadh after drone strike
item.description: Footage shows damage to the US Embassy compound in Riyadh a day after a drone strike caused a small fire and minor structural damage, according to Saudi authorities. The embassy said it would remain closed following the incident.

item.title: Latest news bulletin | March 3rd, 2026 – Midday
item.description: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this March 3rd, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

item.title: Europe must prepare now for the Tehran regime's fall in Iran
item.description: In an opinion piece for Euronews, German SPD politician Danial Ilkhanipour explains why many Iranians see the regime’s end approaching – and fear a deal more than the war.

item.title: Ronaldo's jet leaves Saudi Arabia for Spain after Iran war sparks AFC pause
item.description: Cristiano Ronaldo's private jet left Riyadh for Madrid as the AFC suspended West Region matches amid the Iran war, following drone and missile attacks in the Gulf.

item.title: Workers clear rubble after US-Israeli strikes damage buildings in Tehran
item.description: Machinery removed debris from damaged buildings in Tehran after overnight US-Israeli strikes, as residents described fear during the attacks

item.title: Two rescued from hot-air balloon stranded 274-metres above ground
item.description: Fire crews in Longview, Texas, rescued two people after their hot-air balloon became tangled 274 metres above ground on a communications tower. The hours-long operation involved climbing the structure and lowering them to safety. Both were taken to hospital as a precaution.

item.title: Iraqi police fire tear gas at pro-Iran protest near US embassy in Baghdad
item.description: Police dispersed dozens of Iraqis protesting in support of Iran near Baghdad's Green Zone on Monday night.

item.title: Qatar to extend entry visas for travellers stranded in the country
item.description: The Ministry of Interior has said the visas will automatically be extended for one month.

item.title: Israel deploys additional troops to Lebanon amid Hezbollah attacks
item.description: The death of Iran's Ayatollah Khamenei triggered waves of rocket and drone attacks on Israel by Hezbollah, prompting Israel to respond with airstrikes against the militant group.

item.title: UAE and Qatar to cover hotel and meal costs for those stranded in country during Iran attacks
item.description: Hotels are being asked to extend stays until airport operations resume and tourists are able to leave.

item.title: Building collapses in southern Lebanon after Israeli strike targets Hezbollah-linked institution
item.description: A building collapses and smoke billows into the air as Israel targets offices of Al-Qard Al-Hassan, a Hezbollah-linked financial institution.

item.title: Latest news bulletin | March 3rd, 2026 – Morning
item.description: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this March 3rd, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

item.title: Middle East flights: First departures bring slight relief amid global aviation turmoil
item.description: A small number of Etihad and Emirates flights departed on Monday and Tuesday, but hundreds of thousands of passengers remain in travel limbo.

item.title: US 'totally stupid' to attack Iran during nuclear talks, UN ambassador says
item.description: Air strikes continued across the region on Tuesday as Iran showed no signs of backing down after the US and Israel launched an attack at the weekend that killed Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

item.title: Hundreds of foreign nationals evacuate from Iran to Azerbaijan amid escalating conflict
item.description: Estimates suggest that at least 370-400 people have safely sought refuge in Azerbaijan after crossing the Astara border checkpoint. Some 100 of those were Azerbaijani nationals with the rest being expats from 19 different countries.

item.title: Latest news bulletin | March 2nd, 2026 – Evening
item.description: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this March 2nd, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

item.title: Why the Strait of Hormuz is crucial to global oil and maritime trade
item.description: The Strait of Hormuz is a key shipping lane linking the Gulf to global markets, with a large share of the world’s oil and gas passing through it daily. Disruptions caused by tensions between Iran, the US and Israel risk affecting energy supplies and international trade. Watch the video for more.

item.title: Macron orders nuclear warhead increase and unveils 'forward deterrence' plan for Europe
item.description: Speaking from France’s ballistic-missile submarine base in Brittany, the French president said he “will never hesitate” to protect the country’s “vital interests” and promised “circumstantial deployments” with European allies.

item.title: Tel Aviv residents assess damage after Iranian missile strike kills one
item.description: Residents in Tel Aviv surveyed damage after an Iranian missile strike killed one woman, as sirens and further alerts sent people back to shelters.

item.title: UK says it is not at war after Iranian drone strikes RAF Akrotiri base in Cyprus
item.description: Akrotiri is the UK's main air base for operations in the Middle East and in recent years has been used for missions against the so-called IS group in Syria and Iraq and to strike Houthi targets in Yemen.

item.title: Another oil tanker hit by drone boat as Strait of Hormuz tensions rise
item.description: An oil tanker has been struck near the Strait of Hormuz, marking another attack in the area that triggered an explosion and left one crew member dead.

item.title: Iran war: UAE starts exceptional flights for stranded passengers
item.description: The first flights from the UAE took off just before 3 pm local time on Monday 2 March.

item.title: Latest news bulletin | March 2nd, 2026 – Midday
item.description: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this March 2nd, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

item.title: At least 31 killed as Israel launches strikes on Beirut after Hezbollah rocket fire
item.description: Israeli strikes hit Beirut and other parts of Lebanon after Hezbollah fired rockets and drones toward Israel, with Lebanon’s Health Ministry reporting at least 31 deaths.

item.title: Drones hit Saudi Ras Tanura refinery as Iran strikes targets across region
item.description: Iranian drones hit Saudi Arabia’s Ras Tanura refinery on Monday morning, while Kuwait reported intercepted attacks on day three of Iran's retaliatory strikes on countries in the region.

item.title: People of Iran are ready for freedom and democracy, but the regime has not yet been defeated
item.description: Following the death of Ali Khamenei, the fall of the regime appears to be a realistic option for the first time in 47 years. But the regime still has a monopoly on the use of force. These are historic times for the people of Iran, Gilda Sahebi writes in an opinion article for Eurones.

item.title: Trump says Iran war may last ‘four weeks or less’ as strikes escalate
item.description: Trump says the Iran war could last four weeks or less as US–Israeli forces continued their strikes in Iran while Tehran hits Gulf states, Israel and US targets.

item.title: Missile fire from Iran wounds five in Jerusalem
item.description: Five people were wounded in Jerusalem after missiles fired from Iran struck the area, as cross-border attacks continued for a third day.

item.title: Residents flee Beirut suburbs after Israeli strikes on Hezbollah
item.description: Residents fled Beirut’s southern suburbs after Israeli strikes targeted Hezbollah positions, as tensions linked to the Iran conflict spread to Lebanon.

item.title: Iran's 'terrorist regime' an 'intolerable threat,' Trump says as he outlines war aims
item.description: Tehran has continued its attack against countries in the Middle East on Monday, striking Qatari and Saudi energy facilities and other targets, as Iran war entered its third day. Follow our live blog.

item.title: Latest news bulletin | March 2nd, 2026 – Morning
item.description: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this March 2nd, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

item.title: UK military base in Cyprus targeted by drones as Iran's retaliation widens
item.description: The suspected drone strike came shortly after Prime Minister Keir Starmer said the UK has agreed to let the US use its bases for attacks on Iran’s missiles and their launch sites.

item.title: Iranian missile strike on Beit Shemesh kills at least nine in Israel
item.description: At least nine people were killed after an Iranian missile struck a residential neighborhood in the Israeli city of Beit Shemesh.

item.title: Azerbaijani soldiers are assisting those fleeing the conflict in Iran at the Astara border
item.description: While most are returning Azerbaijanis, 50 foreign nationals, including diplomats, have also used the corridor to evacuate.

item.title: Iranian strikes hit Dubai and Abu Dhabi, damaging airport terminals and the Burj Al Arab
item.description: One person was killed in Abu Dhabi. The UAE claimed to have intercepted hundreds of projectiles as residents sought shelter in basements.

item.title: A new Ayatollah to be chosen within days, Iran's Foreign Minister says
item.description: Retaliatory Iranian strikes killed three US service members and injured several in Israel, as thousands in Yazd mourned, following the death of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
